evals.expected_output: Should find: (1) both nodes have wrong nodeType prefix 'nodes-base.' instead of 'n8n-nodes-base.', (2) chatId on Send Reply is a plain string instead of __rl object, (3) Telegram Trigger has wrong typeVersion 1.1 instead of 1.2, (4) wrong credential key 'telegramAPI' (should be 'telegramApi') on the trigger node
expectations: The output contains the heading '## n8n Workflow Check'
The output identifies both nodes as having the wrong 'nodes-base.' prefix (should be 'n8n-nodes-base.')
The output classifies the incorrect nodeType prefix as an Error, not a Warning
The output identifies chatId on 'Send Reply' as a plain string instead of an __rl object
The output classifies the plain-string chatId as an Error
The output describes or shows the correct __rl format with __rl:true, value, and mode fields
The output reports the typeVersion 1.1 mismatch on Telegram Trigger (expected 1.2)
The output classifies the typeVersion mismatch as a Warning (not an Error)
The output reports the misspelled credential key 'telegramAPI' (should be 'telegramApi')
The output classifies the misspelled credential key as a Warning (wrong case causes runtime auth failure but JSON is structurally valid)
The output offers to apply fixes after presenting the report
The output identifies exactly 4 issues (not more, not fewer)
The output does NOT flag the connections object as having any issues
The output does NOT flag the 'updates: [message]' parameter as wrong

evals.expected_output: Should find: (1) OpenAI Chat Model is wired via 'main' connection instead of 'ai_languageModel' — the LLM won't connect to the agent, (2) Webhook uses responseMode: responseNode but there is no respondToWebhook node in the workflow
expectations: The output identifies that OpenAI Chat Model is wired via 'main' instead of 'ai_languageModel'
The output classifies the wrong AI sub-node connection type as an Error
The output states the correct connection type should be 'ai_languageModel'
The output warns that there is no respondToWebhook node despite responseMode being 'responseNode'
The output does NOT flag the Webhook node's typeVersion or path as issues
The output does NOT flag the AI Agent node's configuration as problematic
The output correctly identifies the OpenAI Chat Model node by name in the finding
The output does not report any false errors on the @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain node types
The output does NOT flag the OpenAI Chat Model node type '@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.lmChatOpenAi' as a wrong prefix
The output does NOT suggest the AI Agent node's text parameter is misconfigured
The output identifies both issues (wrong connection type AND missing respondToWebhook) not just one

evals.expected_output: Should find no errors. This is a clean workflow with correct typeVersions, correct node type prefixes, proper connections, and all required fields present. Should output the clean bill of health message.
expectations: The output does NOT list any Errors for this workflow
The output does NOT falsely flag the httpRequest authentication:none as an error
The output does NOT falsely flag the Code node's jsCode as an error
The output uses the '## n8n Workflow Check' heading
The output either says the workflow is clean/looks good or lists only informational warnings
The output does NOT flag correct typeVersions (1.2, 4.2, 2) as problems
The output explicitly states the workflow passed or has no errors — not just omits errors by silence
The output does NOT flag the absence of credentials on any node as an error (Schedule Trigger and Code require none; HTTP Request uses authentication: none which is explicit)

evals.expected_output: documentId and sheetName are plain strings instead of __rl objects. Both are required resource locator fields for googleSheets node. Should flag both as errors.
expectations: The output identifies documentId as a plain string that should use the __rl format
The output identifies sheetName as a plain string that should use the __rl format
Both documentId and sheetName issues are classified as Errors
The output shows or describes the correct __rl format for documentId
The output shows or describes the correct __rl format for sheetName
The output does NOT flag the googleSheetsOAuth2Api credential key as incorrect
The output does NOT flag typeVersion 4.5 for googleSheets as incorrect
The output correctly identifies the 'Google Sheets' node by name in the findings
The output offers to apply fixes after listing the errors
The output does NOT flag the 'appendOrUpdate' operation name as invalid
The output does NOT flag the missing 'columns' parameter as a critical error (it's optional)

evals.expected_output: Should find: (1) hardcoded Bearer token in 'Call OpenAI' Authorization header is a security risk, (2) Code node uses 'import' statement which is not supported in the Code node sandbox, (3) Code node returns a plain object instead of array of {json} objects
expectations: The output flags the hardcoded Bearer token value in the Authorization header as a security warning
The output classifies the hardcoded secret as a Warning (not a blocking Error)
The output recommends moving the API key to n8n credentials or an expression
The output flags the 'import' statement in jsCode as an Error
The output states that import/export are not supported in the Code node sandbox
The output suggests using require() instead of import
The output flags the return statement returning a plain object instead of [{json: {...}}] as an Error (Code node crashes at runtime — it does not produce wrong output, it produces NO output)
The output does NOT flag the httpRequest URL or method as issues
The output does NOT falsely flag the Webhook node or its configuration
The output does NOT flag authentication:none as an error (the auth is done via headers manually)

evals.expected_output: Should find: (1) two nodes share the name 'Save to Sheets' — duplicate node names cause silent connection failures, (2) IF node only has 1 output wired (true branch) — false branch is missing/unwired
expectations: The output identifies the duplicate node name 'Save to Sheets' as an error
The output classifies the duplicate node name as an Error
The output explains that duplicate names cause connection failures since connections reference nodes by name
The output identifies that the IF node's false branch (output index 1) is not wired
The output classifies the missing IF false branch as an Error
The output does NOT flag the googleSheets __rl fields as issues (they are correctly formatted)
The output does NOT flag the googleSheets typeVersion 4.5 as an issue
The output does NOT flag the IF node condition structure as an error

evals.expected_output: Should find one warning: Memory node has a hardcoded static sessionKey 'support_session' — all users will share the same memory context. Should NOT flag correct ai_languageModel and ai_memory connection types as errors. Should NOT flag the overall structure.
expectations: The output warns about the static/hardcoded sessionKey 'support_session' on the Memory node
The output explains that a static session key causes all executions to share the same memory context
The output suggests using an expression like ={{ $json.message.chat.id }} as the session key instead
The output classifies the static sessionKey as a Warning (not an Error)
The output does NOT flag the ai_languageModel connection as an error
The output does NOT flag the ai_memory connection as an error
The output does NOT flag the overall AI Agent wiring as incorrect
The output does NOT flag the lmOpenAi typeVersion 1.2 as incorrect
The output does NOT flag any __rl fields as issues (chatId is correctly formatted)
The output does NOT flag the absence of a system prompt as a blocking error

evals.expected_output: Should find: (1) HTTP Request uses predefinedCredentialType but has no credentials block — will fail silently on auth, (2) Python Code node imports pandas which is not available in n8n's Python sandbox, (3) Python Code node imports requests which is also not available.
expectations: The output flags that the 'Fetch Data' HTTP Request node has authentication set to predefinedCredentialType but is missing a credentials block
The output classifies the missing credentials block as an Error
The output flags the 'import pandas' statement in the Python code as an Error
The output states that pandas is not available in n8n's Python sandbox
The output flags the 'import requests' statement in the Python code as an Error
The output states that requests is not available and suggests using the HTTP Request node instead
The output does NOT flag the _input.first().json syntax as an error (this is correct Python syntax in n8n)
The output does NOT flag the return format [{'json': row}] as incorrect
The output does NOT flag the manualTrigger typeVersion 1 as an issue
The output does NOT flag the httpRequest typeVersion 4.2 as an issue
